title: Filter methods
key_concepts: Filter methods evaluate features based on their statistical properties, independently of any specific machine learning model.
They act as a fast, scalable pre-processing step, making them ideal for high-dimensional datasets.
Pearson/Spearman correlation measures linear or monotonic relationships between numeric features and the target. Caveat: they completely miss non-linear relationships.
Mutual information measures general dependency, effectively capturing non-linear relationships.
Chi-square tests independence between categorical features and a categorical target.

definitions.Filter methods: Metode seleksi fitur yang menggunakan uji statistik untuk menilai fitur secara independen dari model prediktif.

examples: Explain how a feature with a U-shaped relationship to the target might score zero in Pearson correlation but score highly in mutual information.
relations: Contrasted with wrapper methods in s03.
exclude: Formulas for mutual information and chi-square statistics. Focus on intuition and when to use them.
